text: The glymphatic system, discovered by Maiken Nedergaard at the University of Rochester in 2013, flushes metabolic waste from the brain primarily during sleep — but recent evidence suggests even short wakeful rest periods partially activate this clearance mechanism. During sustained cognitive work, adenosine and other metabolic byproducts accumulate, progressively impairing signal clarity in neural circuits. This is the molecular substrate of mental fatigue — not laziness, not weakness, but a literal accumulation of chemical interference. Brief pauses allow partial clearance, resetting the signal-to-noise ratio in prefrontal circuits and restoring the sharpness of discriminative attention that prolonged work erodes.
question: What does the passage identify as the molecular substrate of mental fatigue?

options.text: Depletion of glucose reserves in prefrontal neurons
Suppression of the glymphatic system by cortisol
Accumulation of adenosine and metabolic byproducts that impair neural signal clarity
Overactivation of the default mode network during sustained work
